sanskrit:
मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा।
निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः॥
transliteration: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-cetasā | nirāśīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ ||
literal_meaning: Having renounced all actions into Me, with the mind centered on the Self, free from expectations and the sense of ‘mine,’ fight, devoid of inner fever.
contextual_meaning: Krishna prescribes a radical inner posture: act fully while psychologically letting go of outcomes, ownership, and egoic entanglement, thereby reducing performance anxiety and moral paralysis.
psychological_state: Flow-like engagement: high agency with low ego involvement; anxiety replaced by focused action.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon must operate on a loved one—skills are demanded, yet emotional detachment is essential for success.
anchor_text: Do your best, then let go—no fear, no claim.
